schema_note: Historical SkillSelect invitation rounds for OT (subclass 189 point-tested + 491 family-sponsored). All cutoffs are FINAL official DHA values unless noted. N/A* = ceiling reached / not invited that round. Range = upper and lower points observed in that round's invite pool (not a single cutoff). For 189 vs 491, both subclasses are tracked. Sources: DHA SkillSelect dashboard, visaiq.com.au, immitrend.com.au, ONEderland Consulting, migrationpages.com.au.

invitation_rounds.ot_cutoff_note: N/A* — occupational ceiling reached, no OT invitations issued in this round
Initial cutoff was 65, lifted to 85 mid-round as ceiling was approached
Cutoff stable — occupation ceiling not reached this round
FY2025-26 round 1 — 6,887 total invitations, OT minimum 80
Range 65-90 — multiple points levels invited in this round
invitation_rounds.source_type: DHA official + Visa Sidekick official list

invitation_rounds.ot_cutoff_note: Family-sponsored stream — 300 total 491 invitations this round
Range 65-85 — preliminary data from SkillSelect Dashboard
invitation_rounds.verify_flag: Single-source — cross-check with DHA official round announcement when published

fy2025_26_metrics.implied_difficulty: extreme — only 1,953 of 11,498 EOIs (17%) meet the 85-point floor, but only 1,027 spots remain

trend_analysis.cutoff_direction_summary: OT 189 cutoffs: 85 (Jun 24) → 75 (Sep 24) → 75 (Nov 24) → 80 (Aug 25) → 75 (Nov 25). The 80 spike in Aug 25 likely reflects FY-start catch-up demand. Net trend: stable around 75-80 with periodic spikes to 85.
trend_analysis.demand_signal: Active EOIs 11,498 with only 1,027 FY spots remaining → 11.2:1 EOI-to-spot ratio. OT is currently one of the most oversubscribed occupations.
trend_analysis.ceiling_reached_history: May 2023 (FY22-23) — no OT issued; ceiling reached before OT candidates

implications_for_applicant.recommended_target_score: 90+ to be safely above the floor
implications_for_applicant.tie_break_advantage: Lodge EOI as early as possible (date-of-effect is tie-breaker for equal scores)
implications_for_applicant.alternative_pathway: 491 (state-nominated) and 190 are less competitive if the applicant is willing to live in a regional/state-nominated area

caveats_and_limitations: DHA publishes 189 round results in batches; some rounds from late 2025 / early 2026 may not yet be in this dataset
OT-specific invitation counts are rarely published — only the minimum points score per round is officially published
491 family-sponsored stream uses different eligibility; 491 state-nominated invitations are issued continuously by states/territories and are NOT in this dataset
The 65 → 85 cutoff shift in June 2024 is a known SkillSelect pattern: initial cutoff = top-ranked pool, then lifts as more invitations are issued against the ceiling
All data is INFERRED from publicly republished DHA announcements. Verify against https://immi.homeaffairs.gov.au/visas/working-in-australia/skillselect/previous-rounds for the final word
